notes.toolchain_choice: Option C — swiftc -typecheck per file with published AmitySDK 8.1.1 xcframework binary. Option A skipped (no pre-built xcframework in AmitySDKIOS source repo). Option B attempted but blocked by SwiftPM libgit2 safe.bareRepository policy on this host (global git config is ignored by SwiftPM's embedded libgit2). Option C works cleanly: 0.2s per snippet, no SPM dependency graph, tests against the same binary users install.
top_failure_categories: PREAMBLE GAPS — 'client', 'channelRepository', 'messageRepository', 'postRepository', 'userRepository', 'fileRepository', 'communityRepository', 'token' not declared in preamble. Docs assume these are already initialised context vars. Fix: expand preamble to include repository instances.

RENAMED/MISSING TYPES — 'AmityChannelQuery', 'AmityChannelUpdateBuilder', 'AmityChannelParticipation', 'AmitySessionHandler', 'AmityUserUpdateBuilder', 'AmityError' not found. These may be renamed or removed in v8.1.1 vs what the docs describe.
UIKit IN SCOPE — 'UIImage' used in image-handling block fails because UIKit requires a valid SDK. Fix: add -framework UIKit to compiler flags.
FILE-SCOPE DECLARATIONS — some blocks contain class/struct declarations that are only valid at file scope, not inside a function body. Extractor wraps them in a function — these need skip markers.
ACTUAL API DRIFT — 'completion:' handler in login call vs 'sessionHandler:' in v8.1.1 is real drift. 'getLivePlaybackUrl' gone from AmityRoom. 'AmityObject.object' property renamed.
ios_quirks: Combine-based APIs use AnyCancellable / sink — compile fine in async context
AmityObject<T> wrapper pattern is iOS-specific; .object renamed to .value or similar in v8
UIKit dependency in image snippets requires -framework UIKit flag (not added in v1)
Session handler pattern (AmitySessionState etc) differs significantly between docs and published SDK
notes.preamble_gap_vs_drift: Most failures (50+) are preamble gaps — undeclared local vars that snippets assume exist. These are NOT API drift; they just need a richer preamble. True drift candidates: completion: vs sessionHandler:, getLivePlaybackUrl, AmityObject.object. Estimated real drift: ~5-8 blocks. Preamble fix alone could bring pass rate to ~75%.
